beginnerGuide.title: Grand Alfheim Beginner Guide
beginnerGuide.intro: Grand Alfheim combines open-world exploration, class-based weapon combat, racial magic, flight, quests, bosses, and dungeons. Follow this route to create your character, learn combat, choose a class, complete starter quests, and prepare for the Temple of Beginnings.

steps.title: Create Your Character
steps.goal: Choose a race and appearance that match your preferred fantasy and combat style.
actions: Select one of the nine fairy races: Undine, Spriggan, Leprechaun, Imp, Cait Sith, Sylph, Salamander, Pooka, or Gnome.
Review the race's associated magic and specialty before confirming your character.
Finish appearance customization and enter the tutorial area.
steps.result: Your character is ready to begin the tutorial and select a combat class.

steps.title: Complete the Tutorial
steps.goal: Learn the movement and defensive mechanics used throughout the game.
actions: Practice movement with W, A, S, and D and use M1 for basic weapon attacks.
Learn how to run, roll, block, interact with NPCs, and activate equipped skills.
Practice flying with V because many quests and territories require vertical travel.
Open the main menu with M to review equipment, skills, quests, and character progression.
steps.result: You can move safely through the open world and avoid relying only on basic attacks.

steps.title: Choose a Starter Class
steps.goal: Select a weapon style that is easy to use while learning enemy attack patterns.
actions: Visit the forge or blacksmith and choose a class such as Swordsman, Duelist, Assassin, Pugilist, Titanblade, or Aegis.
Use the corresponding starter weapon provided when selecting the class.
Test the weapon's attack speed, range, stamina cost, and class skills against nearby enemies.
Return to a blacksmith to change classes when another weapon style suits you better.
steps.result: You have a class weapon and a clear combat role for early quests.

steps.title: Finish Starter Quests
steps.goal: Earn early levels, currency, equipment, and skill progression.
actions: Prioritize main quests because they introduce important locations and progression systems.
Complete nearby side quests while traveling between main objectives.
Accept class quests that improve your understanding of the selected weapon.
Defeat enemies near your level instead of repeatedly fighting stronger monsters.
steps.result: Your character reaches the early level range required for stronger enemies and the first dungeon.

steps.title: Improve Skills and Equipment
steps.goal: Build enough damage and survivability for group content.
actions: Spend class points on a small group of frequently used abilities instead of unlocking every branch immediately.
Develop your magic tree alongside the class tree so that weapon and magic attacks can be combined.
Equip weapons and armor that meet your current level and class requirements.
Use flight for short routes and a mount for convenient ground travel across large territories.
steps.result: You have a functional combat rotation and level-appropriate equipment.

steps.title: Practice Dungeon Combat
steps.goal: Prepare for groups of enemies, puzzles, and boss mechanics.
actions: Practice pulling small enemy groups instead of allowing several enemies to surround you.
Save high-cooldown skills for dangerous enemies or boss openings.
Learn when to dodge, block, retreat, and recover stamina.
Join other players when your damage, defense, or knowledge of the route is still limited.
steps.result: You can survive longer encounters without exhausting every skill at the start.

steps.title: Clear the Temple of Beginnings
steps.goal: Complete the first major dungeon and begin farming stronger equipment.
actions: Enter at approximately level 10 with upgraded starter equipment.
Choose the left parkour route or the right combat route containing groups of monsters.
Complete the dungeon's cube puzzles to open the route toward the final encounter.
Defeat the Bone Dragon and repeat the dungeon for class weapons, armor, and other rewards.
steps.result: You have completed your first dungeon and unlocked the main equipment-farming loop.

raceTierList.title: Grand Alfheim Race Tier List
raceTierList.intro: This ranking favors races with broadly useful combat or progression specialties. Salamander, Sylph, and Undine provide the clearest offensive, mobility, and support identities, while the remaining races are stronger when used for a specific build or team role.

tiers.label: Best All-Round Choices
tiers.description: Strong identities that remain useful during quests, dungeons, boss fights, and group play.

races.name: Salamander
races.bestFor: Players who want an aggressive melee or damage-focused build.

races.name: Sylph
strengths: Useful for exploration and mobile combat
races.bestFor: Players who prefer speed, spacing, and frequent movement.

races.name: Undine
races.bestFor: Support players and groups progressing through difficult PvE content.

tiers.label: Strong Specialist Choices
tiers.description: Powerful choices for mobility, utility, crowd control, or coordinated team builds.

races.name: Cait Sith
races.bestFor: Mobile players interested in companions, summoning, and open-world exploration.

races.name: Spriggan
races.bestFor: Players who prefer utility, deception, and finding valuable rewards.

races.name: Pooka
races.bestFor: Group players who want to strengthen allies and interfere with enemies.

tiers.label: Focused Utility Choices
tiers.description: Useful racial identities that work best with a planned build or specialized activity.

races.name: Gnome
strengths: Natural fit for heavy or defensive builds
races.bestFor: Players building around durability, heavy weapons, or frontline combat.

races.name: Imp
races.bestFor: Players who prefer status effects, nighttime exploration, and disruptive magic.

races.name: Leprechaun
races.bestFor: Players interested in crafting, equipment, and a technology-inspired fairy style.

classesAndBuilds.title: Grand Alfheim Classes and Builds
classesAndBuilds.intro: Classes are selected through the forge or a blacksmith and each class uses its own weapon category and skill tree. Selecting a class provides its corresponding starter weapon, while returning to a blacksmith lets players test another fighting style without creating a new character.

classes.name: Swordsman
classes.combatStyle: Balanced close-range attacks with dependable reach and straightforward combos.
classes.buildPriority: Prioritize weapon damage and core class skills, then add enough durability for extended fights.
classes.recommendedFor: Beginners, solo quests, and balanced PvE progression.

classes.name: Bowmaster
classes.combatStyle: Ranged attacks that reward accuracy, positioning, charged shots, and distance control.
classes.buildPriority: Prioritize DEX, ranged damage, accuracy, and abilities that improve charged or multi-projectile attacks.
classes.recommendedFor: Safe PvE damage, ranged boss pressure, and players who avoid close combat.

classes.name: Pugilist
classes.combatStyle: Fast close-range pressure built around short attack distance and continuous combos.
classes.buildPriority: Invest in damage, stamina management, and skills that extend or finish close-range combos.
classes.recommendedFor: Aggressive players who enjoy staying directly beside the target.

classes.name: Assassin
classes.combatStyle: Fast stabbing attacks, close-range burst damage, mobility, and poison pressure.
classes.buildPriority: Combine DEX and STR with mobility skills and damage-over-time effects such as poison.
classes.recommendedFor: PvP pressure, fast enemy elimination, and high-mobility builds.

classes.name: Crescentmaster
classes.combatStyle: Wide sweeping attacks that use the scythe's long handle and curved blade to control space.
classes.buildPriority: Prioritize STR, attack reach, and area attacks that can damage multiple nearby enemies.
classes.recommendedFor: Enemy groups, area control, and unconventional heavy-weapon play.

classes.name: Duelist
classes.combatStyle: Quick thrusting attacks, precise multi-hit skills, and rapid footwork.
classes.buildPriority: Prioritize DEX, attack speed, precision, and skills that create fast thrusting combinations.
classes.recommendedFor: Mobile PvP, single-target damage, and players who value speed over heavy hits.

classes.name: Titanblade
classes.combatStyle: Slow but powerful heavy attacks with strong impact, wide swings, and area pressure.
classes.buildPriority: Stack STR and improve heavy class skills while maintaining enough stamina and defense to complete attack animations.
classes.recommendedFor: High-damage PvE, grouped enemies, and players comfortable with slower weapons.

classes.name: Aegis
classes.combatStyle: Defensive frontline combat using a large shield to block, deflect, absorb, and return damage.
classes.buildPriority: Prioritize STR, durability, blocking, and skills that protect allies or control enemy attention.
classes.recommendedFor: Dungeon groups, boss tanking, and defensive team play.

classes.name: Sentinel
classes.combatStyle: Controlled attacks focused on spacing, consistent pressure, and deliberate skill timing.
classes.buildPriority: Develop the class skill tree first and balance weapon damage with survivability and stamina.
classes.recommendedFor: Players who prefer measured combat instead of constant close-range aggression.

classes.name: Skull Crusher
classes.combatStyle: Heavy blunt attacks designed for large impact, enemy interruption, and powerful single hits.
classes.buildPriority: Prioritize STR, heavy-attack damage, stamina, and durability.
classes.recommendedFor: Heavy melee builds and players who prefer impact over attack speed.

classes.name: Berserker
classes.combatStyle: Aggressive frontline fighting that trades speed and safety for sustained offensive pressure.
classes.buildPriority: Build around STR, damage output, stamina, and enough defense to remain in close combat.
classes.recommendedFor: High-risk melee players, enemy farming, and offensive group roles.

levelingProgression.title: Grand Alfheim Leveling Guide
levelingProgression.intro: Efficient progression comes from combining main quests, nearby side quests, class development, magic skills, enemy farming, and dungeon equipment. Move into the next stage when regular enemies can be defeated consistently without consuming all of your stamina or high-cooldown abilities.

content: Complete the full tutorial
Choose a class at the forge
Equip the starter class weapon
Practice basic attacks, rolling, blocking, skills, and flight
stages.goal: Understand your controls and defeat nearby starter enemies without repeatedly being knocked down.
stages.readyWhen: You can complete simple fights while preserving stamina and health.

content: Follow main quests through the starting territory
Complete side and class quests found along the route
Defeat nearby enemy groups for experience and drops
Invest early points into a small, reliable class rotation
Begin developing the magic skill tree
stages.goal: Reach approximately level 10 with upgraded equipment and several usable combat skills.
stages.readyWhen: Normal enemies near the first dungeon route can be defeated consistently.

content: Enter the Temple of Beginnings alone or with a group
Use the combat route for fighting practice or the parkour route for faster movement
Defeat Scorpions, Dinosaurs, plant enemies, tree enemies, and other dungeon monsters
Complete the cube puzzles
Defeat the Bone Dragon
Farm level 10 and level 15 class equipment
stages.goal: Replace starter gear with dungeon weapons or armor and become comfortable with boss mechanics.
stages.readyWhen: You can finish the dungeon route and contribute reliable damage against the final boss.

content: Continue main and side quests across additional race territories
Fight stronger open-world enemies and territory bosses
Repeat the Temple of Beginnings for missing class equipment
Improve both class and magic skill trees
Test upgraded abilities against groups instead of isolated starter enemies
stages.goal: Build a complete early-game equipment set and a dependable weapon-and-magic rotation.
stages.readyWhen: Early dungeon enemies and common territory monsters no longer slow progression.

content: Finish remaining questlines in unlocked territories
Farm bosses and dungeon encounters for higher-rarity equipment
Refine class skill allocation instead of spreading points across unused abilities
Practice coordinated combat with other players
Prepare a damage, support, or frontline role for advanced group content
stages.goal: Reach level 50 with a focused build, upgraded equipment, and experience handling boss phases.
stages.readyWhen: Your character can survive long encounters and perform a clear role within a party.

stages.stage: Ode to the Abyss and Endgame Farming
content: Form a group of approximately four to eight prepared players
Enter Ode to the Abyss during its scheduled opening window
Learn the boss's multiple combat phases
Destroy the four activated towers quickly during the low-health phase
Repeat the encounter for high-level and Mythical equipment
Continue refining separate PvE and PvP builds
stages.goal: Farm endgame equipment and complete the strongest available group encounters.
stages.readyWhen: Your group can handle the boss's phase changes, tower mechanic, and sustained damage requirements.

weaponsAndArmor.title: Grand Alfheim Weapons and Armor
weaponsAndArmor.intro: Grand Alfheim weapons are tied to combat classes, while armor provides additional health through mask, top, and bottom slots. Blacksmith weapons support early progression, Temple of Beginnings gear covers the first major upgrades, and Ode to the Abyss contains level 50 Mythical equipment.

items.role: Balanced starter weapon with straightforward melee strings.
Ranged pressure, safer questing, and enemy kiting.
Quick melee strings and short punish windows.
Slower attacks with strong impact and reach.
Mid-game sword progression before level 50 equipment.
High-level PvE and PvP sword builds.
High-level ranged damage and battlefield control.
Adds 120 HP from the complete set for early dungeon progression.
Adds 820 HP from the complete set for more demanding PvE encounters.

dungeonsBosses.title: Grand Alfheim Dungeons, Bosses, and Loot
dungeonsBosses.intro: Dungeons combine combat routes, puzzles, minibosses, and final encounters. Bring healing items, learn the block and parry system, and use a class that can reliably damage bosses without exhausting its stamina.

encounters.name: Temple of Beginnings
encounters.entry: Recommended level 10+. Entrances are available from Salamander, Undine, and Gnome territories.
encounters.pattern: Choose the left parkour route or the right monster route. Both paths lead to rooms where cubes must be placed on matching angel and demon altars before the boss portal opens.
encounters.preparation: Bring healing items and practice parrying before entering. The monster route provides more combat practice, while the parkour route avoids several enemy groups.

encounters.name: Treant
encounters.entry: Encountered inside the Temple of Beginnings during dungeon progression.
encounters.pattern: Treant uses heavy close-range attacks that reward timed blocks and parries. Avoid spending all stamina on attacks before its next swing.
encounters.preparation: Use short attack strings, keep enough stamina to defend, and punish after a successful parry or completed boss animation.

encounters.name: Bone Dragon
encounters.entry: Reached after completing a dungeon route and solving the cube rooms.
encounters.pattern: Fight inside the final arena and stay outside the boss's direct attack line until an animation finishes. Dash away from large attacks instead of extending a melee combo.
encounters.preparation: Enter with repaired equipment, healing supplies, and a complete skill loadout. Groups should spread around the arena so the boss does not pressure every player at once.

encounters.name: Ode to the Abyss
encounters.entry: The chained entrance is located in Gnome territory. It opens at 12:00 AM and 12:00 PM Eastern Time for a 10-minute entry window.
encounters.pattern: Use the dungeon elevator to reach Valdrayn. During the low-health phase, four towers activate and must be destroyed before returning damage to the boss.
encounters.preparation: Recommended level 50+ with a coordinated group. Assign players to separate towers, save high-damage skills for the tower phase, and keep healing resources for the final section.

encounters.name: Satyr
encounters.pattern: Satyr fights at close range with committed melee attacks. Block the opening pressure, dash away from heavy swings, and attack during recovery animations.
encounters.preparation: Use a weapon with a comfortable attack speed and avoid long combos while the boss is ready to counterattack.

controlsAndCombat.title: Grand Alfheim Controls and Combat
controlsAndCombat.intro: Grand Alfheim supports PC, mobile, and console. PC has fixed keyboard and mouse inputs, while mobile and console display matching touch or controller prompts through the in-game interface. Use feints, parries, critical attacks, and movement attacks to create safer openings.

controls.usage: Walk around towns, dungeons, and combat arenas.
Perform the standard weapon combo. Stop the string early when an enemy is preparing to counter.
Cancel or disguise an attack to draw out an opponent's block, parry, or dodge.
Hold to block. Press shortly before an incoming hit to parry and create a counterattack window.
Create distance, avoid heavy attacks, or begin a dash attack by following the dash with M1.
Use the weapon's critical attack when the enemy is recovering, stunned, or low on defensive stamina.
Attack while airborne to change timing and continue pressure from a jump.
Close distance with an immediate attack after dashing.
Summon the equipped mount when outside restricted combat areas.
Activate race wings and begin aerial travel when flight is available.
Use the labeled melee, feint, block, dash, critical, mount, flight, and skill buttons displayed on the screen.
Move with the joystick and drag the right side of the screen to control the camera and targeting direction.
Follow the current controller icons for melee, block, dash, critical attacks, skills, mounts, and flight.
Watch each cooldown icon before committing to another combo or boss phase.

updatesEarlyAccess.title: Grand Alfheim Updates and Early Access
updatesEarlyAccess.intro: Grand Alfheim has progressed through multiple testing phases covering combat, mounts, flight, classes, maps, survival systems, dungeons, and public access. The official pre-release demo opened in July 2026 with a summer event and increased drop rewards.

entries.title: Mount and Flight Systems
highlights: Horse mounts added
Race flight and flight assistance added
Cosmetic armor and settings added
Dynamic lighting introduced
Mobile flight support improved

entries.title: Combat and Class Improvements
highlights: Updated Blacksmith and class-selection interface
Skill cooldown indicators added
Parry timing and combat feedback improved
Weapon attack speeds adjusted
Mobile and controller interface fixes added

entries.title: Weapon Class Expansion
highlights: A new weapon class joined the combat roster
Additional progression and equipment options were introduced
Testing continued for class balance and weapon speed

entries.title: World and Survival Update
highlights: Gnome territory received a major map revamp
Hunger and cooking systems were added
Dungeon and boss progression expanded
New equipment and high-level rewards were introduced

entries.title: Summer Demo Announcement
highlights: The official Grand Alfheim account confirmed a Summer 2026 pre-release demo
Players were directed to official community channels for access news

entries.title: Grand Alfheim Public Demo Opens
highlights: The official Roblox experience opened as a public pre-release demo
The summer demo included a 2X Drops event
The White Room activity offered a route to earn early-access participation
PC, mobile, and console support were listed on the official game page

entries.title: Official Announcements and Testing Events
highlights: Discord publishes updates, sneak peeks, events, and giveaways
The Roblox groups connect players to official experiences
The official social account posts demo and release announcements

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Grand Alfheim before you play

questions.question: What is Grand Alfheim?
questions.answer: Grand Alfheim is a Sword Art Online-inspired fantasy MMORPG on Roblox. It features large open-world exploration, nine fairy races, multiple combat classes, flight, quests, bosses, dungeons, and both PvE and PvP content.

questions.question: Is Grand Alfheim free to play?
questions.answer: Yes. Grand Alfheim is a Roblox experience, so you can play it for free through the official Roblox game page on PC, mobile, and console.

questions.question: What is the current development stage of Grand Alfheim?
questions.answer: Grand Alfheim is in Early Access as a public pre-release demo. The official demo opened in July 2026 and includes a summer 2X Drops event for players joining the experience.

questions.question: Which race should I pick in Grand Alfheim?
questions.answer: Salamander, Sylph, and Undine are the strongest all-round choices for offense, mobility, and support. Other races such as Cait Sith, Spriggan, and Gnome shine when built around a specific playstyle or team role.

questions.question: How do I change my class in Grand Alfheim?
questions.answer: Visit the forge or a blacksmith to select a new class. Each class provides its own starter weapon and skill tree, and you can switch classes at a blacksmith without creating a new character.

questions.question: How do I unlock flight in Grand Alfheim?
questions.answer: Flight is tied to your race wings and is activated with the V key on PC. Many quests and territories require vertical travel, so practice flying early during the tutorial.

questions.question: Where do I find Grand Alfheim codes and updates?
questions.answer: Codes, updates, events, and announcements are shared through the official Grand Alfheim Discord, the Roblox group, and the official X account. Always use these official channels for access news.
